rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope before use.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the actual types of expressions.
Validate that function calls provide the correct number of arguments as defined in their signatures.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure type consistency between expressions and their expected types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in annex expressions to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
When defining constants or equations, use correct AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE requires binary expressions to have matching left and right types; use type alignment to prevent '<error>' type mismatches.
Function calls in AGREE must match the number and types of parameters defined in the function signature.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing undeclared or non-visible identifiers in AGREE annexes; this leads to 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
Do not mix AADL package-qualified names in AGREE unless explicitly allowed by the model's with clauses.
